defaultRegimeNote: New tax regime u/s 115BAC is the DEFAULT for FY 2025-26 per Finance Act 2025. Taxpayers must explicitly opt for old regime by filing Form 10-IEA before or at the time of filing ITR.
legalReference: Section 115BAC, Income-tax Act 1961 (as amended by Finance Act 2025)

new.label: New Tax Regime (Default — Section 115BAC)
new.optOutNote: Salaried/other individuals simply choose regime at filing; no separate form needed unless they have business income.
new.basicExemptionLimitNote: ₹4,00,000 — nil tax up to this amount under new regime slabs (FY 2025-26 revision from earlier ₹3L)

rebateU87A.note: Full income tax rebate u/s 87A if total taxable income does not exceed ₹12,00,000. Rebate = lower of tax payable or ₹60,000. Effectively zero tax up to ₹12,00,000. For salaried with standard deduction of ₹75,000, salary up to ₹12,75,000 is tax-free.
rebateU87A.legalReference: Section 87A, Income-tax Act 1961 (amended by Finance Act 2025)

standardDeduction.note: Standard deduction of ₹75,000 available for salaried and pensioners under new regime (₹50,000 under old regime). Family pension: standard deduction is lower of ₹15,000 or 1/3rd of family pension.

slabs.changeNote: Nil slab extended from ₹3L to ₹4L in FY 2025-26 (Finance Act 2025)
New slab introduced in FY 2025-26 (Finance Act 2025)
New slab introduced in FY 2025-26 (Finance Act 2025)
New slab introduced in FY 2025-26 (Finance Act 2025)

surcharge.note: 10% surcharge on income tax if total income > ₹50L and ≤ ₹1Cr
15% surcharge on income tax if total income > ₹1Cr and ≤ ₹2Cr
25% surcharge (capped at 25% under new regime — unlike old regime where it was 37% for income > ₹5Cr). Applicable from ₹2Cr onwards.

deductionsAllowed: Employer NPS contribution u/s 80CCD(2) — up to 14% of salary (central/state govt employees) or 10% otherwise
deductionsNotAllowed: Interest on housing loan u/s 24(b) for self-occupied property
Set-off of house property loss against other income

old.label: Old Tax Regime (Optional — opt via Form 10-IEA for business income; salaried can choose at filing)
old.optInForm: Form 10-IEA (only for taxpayers with business/professional income; salaried/others simply select at time of ITR filing)

rebateU87A.note: Rebate u/s 87A under old regime: if total taxable income ≤ ₹5,00,000, tax is reduced by rebate amount (max ₹12,500). Tax is zero for income up to ₹5L (for individuals below 60 years).

surcharge.note: 37% surcharge for income > ₹5 Crore under old regime (unlike new regime where max surcharge is capped at 25%)

old.deductionsAllowed: All deductions under Chapter VI-A (80C, 80D, 80E, 80G, etc.), HRA, LTA, housing loan interest u/s 24(b), house property loss set-off

ltcg112A.description: LTCG on listed equity shares / equity-oriented MF units / units of business trust
ltcg112A.exemptionNote: First ₹1,25,000 of LTCG u/s 112A is exempt per year (₹1 lakh up to FY 2023-24; raised to ₹1.25 lakh from FY 2024-25 by Finance Act 2024)
ltcg112A.legalReference: Section 112A, Income-tax Act 1961 (amended by Finance Act 2024)
ltcg112A.fy202526Note: Rate increased from 10% to 12.5% by Finance Act 2024, effective for transfers from July 23, 2024. Applicable in FY 2025-26.

stcg111A.description: STCG on listed equity shares / equity-oriented MF units / units of business trust (STT paid)
stcg111A.legalReference: Section 111A, Income-tax Act 1961 (amended by Finance Act 2024)
stcg111A.fy202526Note: Rate increased from 15% to 20% by Finance Act 2024, effective for transfers from July 23, 2024.

ltcgOtherAssets.description: LTCG on other assets (immovable property, unlisted shares, debt MF, etc.)
ltcgOtherAssets.indexationBenefitNote: Indexation removed by Finance Act 2024 for most assets. Exception: For immovable property acquired before July 23, 2024, taxpayer can opt for 20% with indexation OR 12.5% without indexation — whichever is lower.
ltcgOtherAssets.holdingPeriod: More than 24 months (immovable property/unlisted shares); 36 months (other assets)
ltcgOtherAssets.legalReference: Section 112, Income-tax Act 1961 (amended by Finance Act 2024)

stcgOtherAssets.description: STCG on other assets (immovable property, debt MF, etc.)
stcgOtherAssets.note: Short-term capital gains on assets not covered u/s 111A are taxed at the applicable income tax slab rate

virtualDigitalAssets.description: Income from transfer of Virtual Digital Assets (crypto, NFTs, etc.)
virtualDigitalAssets.note: 30% flat tax + 4% cess = 31.2% effective rate. No deductions except cost of acquisition. Losses cannot be set off or carried forward.

domesticCompany.note: Companies with turnover ≤ ₹400 Cr in FY 2020-21: 25%. New manufacturing companies u/s 115BAB: 15%. Section 115BAA (all companies): 22% (no incentives/deductions). All rates before surcharge and cess.

mat115JB.note: Minimum Alternate Tax at 15% of book profit if regular tax < 15% of book profit. Not applicable to companies opting for Section 115BAA/115BAB.
